hypothesis: If frozen single-gas neural networks are used to generate gas-specific features that are combined in a GraphSAGE model for Ar/O2 mixtures, then the model will predict 10-bin radial PMMA etching rates with lower test MSE (higher R²) and better generalization to withheld high power/pressure (Testgrid) and unseen low-xAr (Testthresh) conditions than FNN, GCN, or GAT baselines because composable single-gas latent representations capture transferable gas-specific plasma physics.
